name: 3D Geometry — Lines and Planes in Space
whatIsIt: In space, a LINE is fixed by a POINT on it plus a DIRECTION. That direction is described by DIRECTION COSINES l, m, n (the cosines of the angles the line makes with the x, y, z axes) which always satisfy l² + m² + n² = 1. A PLANE has an equation ax + by + cz = d, and the vector (a, b, c) is its NORMAL — the direction sticking straight out of the plane.
whyNeeded: Robotics, CAD, aircraft paths, ray-tracing in graphics, and structural engineering all need the exact equations of lines and flat surfaces in 3D. This is where vectors and 3D coordinates become powerful tools.
realLifeUses: Aircraft and drone flight paths in space
Walls and roofs as planes in architecture/CAD
Light rays hitting surfaces in 3D graphics

vocabulary.meaning: The cosines of the angles a line makes with the x, y, z axes; l² + m² + n² = 1.
Fixed by one point on it and a direction (vector).
A flat surface; the vector (a, b, c) is its normal.
The direction perpendicular to a plane, read from its equation as (a, b, c).

story.title: The Drone Flight Path
story.text: Vivaan programs a drone to fly in a straight line. 'What do you need to fix that line?' asks the Instructor. 'A point it passes through, and a direction!' Then they set a landing pad as the plane 2x + 3y + z = 5. 'The direction straight out of the pad is its normal, (2, 3, 1),' the Instructor says, reading it right off the equation.
story.extractedProblem: For the plane 2x + 3y + z = 5, what is the normal vector?
story.answerInStory: (2, 3, 1) — the coefficients of x, y, z give the normal.

shapes.label: plane ax+by+cz=d with normal (a,b,c); a line = point + direction

visual.caption: A plane's equation ax + by + cz = d hides its normal (a, b, c). A line is set by a point plus a direction with cosines l, m, n (l²+m²+n²=1).

teachingGallery.title: Direction and normal
teachingGallery.note: Direction cosines square-sum to 1; a plane's normal is read from its equation.

examples.caption: A line needs a point on it and a direction vector.
Direction cosines always satisfy l² + m² + n² = 1.
Read the normal (2, 3, 1) straight from the plane's coefficients.

standardMethod.summary: A line = a point + a direction; direction cosines obey l² + m² + n² = 1. A plane ax + by + cz = d has normal (a, b, c). Use dot products for angles.
steps: To fix a line, give one point on it and a direction vector.
Direction cosines l, m, n are the axis-angle cosines; check l² + m² + n² = 1.
A plane's equation is ax + by + cz = d.
Its normal vector is (a, b, c) — read straight from the coefficients.
Find angles between lines/planes using the dot product of their direction/normal vectors.

workedExamples.problem: For the plane 2x + 3y + z = 5, what is the x-component of the normal vector?
steps: The normal is (a, b, c) = the coefficients.

workedExamples.problem: Direction cosines satisfy l² + m² + n² = ?
steps: Direction cosines are cosines of the axis angles.
Their squares always add to one.

commonMistakes.mistake: Forgetting that direction cosines must satisfy l² + m² + n² = 1.
commonMistakes.fix: Direction cosines ALWAYS square-sum to 1. If yours don't, they aren't proper direction cosines yet — normalise them.

commonMistakes.mistake: Taking the constant d as part of the normal, or misreading the coefficients.
commonMistakes.fix: The normal is (a, b, c) — the coefficients of x, y, z only. The constant d is not part of it.

easy.q: How many direction cosines does a line in space have?

medium.q: Plane 2x + 3y + z = 5. x-component of the normal (2,3,1)?

challenge.q: A student says the direction cosines of a line can add (as squares) to 2. What's the fix?

options.label: They must satisfy l² + m² + n² = 1

challenge.answer: They must satisfy l² + m² + n² = 1

questions.q: Number of direction cosines for a 3D line?
Plane x + 7y + 2z = 4. y-component of the normal?
The normal to the plane ax + by + cz = d is…

teachBackPrompt: Explain to a grown-up that a line in space needs a point plus a direction, that direction cosines satisfy l²+m²+n² = 1, and that the plane 2x+3y+z = 5 has normal (2, 3, 1) read from its coefficients.

revisionCard.summary: Line in space = a point + a direction. Direction cosines l, m, n (axis-angle cosines) satisfy l²+m²+n² = 1. Plane: ax+by+cz = d, with normal vector (a, b, c) (coefficients only; not d). Use dot products for angles between lines/planes.
